items.title: Terror accused charged with child abuse, extremist material offences

children.content: A man accused of planning terrorist attacks in Perth is charged with further offences, including possession of child abuse material and violent extremist material on his devices.

gallery.altText: A man smiles at a camera with a digger behind him

thumbnail.altText: A man smiles at a camera with a digger behind him

items.title: E-scooter rider dies after being hit by car while crossing Perth highway

children.content: A man in his 40s is struck by a hatchback and killed while riding an e-scooter across Reid Highway in Perth's northern suburbs.

image.altText: A generic photograph of an unidentified WA Police officer wearing a high visibility police vest over a blue uniform.

items.title: Women's refuge turns to smarter solutions to fight tracking technology

children.content: A women's shelter in regional Western Australia has had to adapt to an increase in technology-facilitated violence to keep victims safe.

items.title: Solar eclipse to attract thousands to 'middle of nowhere' in 2028

children.content: One of the longest solar eclipses this century, albeit for five minutes, is set to put the remote East Kimberley on the world map.

gallery.altText: A child wearing eclipse glasses looks up at the sky
blonde woman with blue top posing next to an information sign

thumbnail.altText: A child wearing eclipse glasses looks up at the sky
Artemis II — a sunrise for orion
blonde woman with blue top posing next to an information sign

items.title: Father allegedly high on 'nangs' in crash that killed daughters, as scuffle erupts outside court

children.content: A Perth father was allegedly high on 'nangs' during a crash that killed his two daughters, as a scuffle with media erupts at court.

image.altText: A wide shot of the front facade and entrance to the Midland Courthouse.

items.title: Murdoch Uni admits accessibility needs fix after student's viral video

children.content: The former Murdoch University student's video highlights the difficulty wheelchair users have navigating the campus since stair lifts were removed in 2023.

image.altText: Young woman with curly brown hair sitting in wheelchair

items.title: Record payout after billionaire built mine without traditional owners' permission

children.content: The Yindjibarndi people win an historic compensation payout in the Federal Court, after Andrew Forrest's Fortescue Metals Group built mines on their land without their permission.

gallery.altText: A composite image of Andrew Forrest (left) and Michael Woodley situated at a Fortescue mine in WA.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of Andrew Forrest (left) and Michael Woodley situated at a Fortescue mine in WA.

items.title: Hantavirus ship travellers headed to Perth's COVID 'white elephant'

children.content: Nestled away in Perth's outer fringes, this 500-bed facility was built during the COVID-19 pandemic as a quarantine centre but sat idle for years. Now, a different deadly virus will see it used for its intended purpose.

articles.title: British paratroopers drop onto remote island after suspected hantavirus case

children.content: The UK has ordered 10 people from remote island territories go into isolation, while parachuting in medical supplies, clinicians over suspected hantavirus case.

image.altText: A paratrooper with equipment strapped to his front jumps out of an airplane.

articles.title: Six passengers from hantavirus cruise ship 'in good health', minister says

children.content: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of the deadly hantavirus outbreak are "in good health", as the Australian government remains hopeful it will finalise plans to repatriate them within 48 hours.

image.altText: A passenger on board the cruise ship MV Hondius

gallery.altText: The exterior of a quarantine facility on the outskirts of Perth.
An aerial photo of a construction site
Two single beds inside the Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre.

thumbnail.altText: The exterior of a quarantine facility on the outskirts of Perth.
An aerial photo of a construction site
Two single beds inside the Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre.

items.title: Eurovision pays musical homage to Austria-Australia confusion

children.content: The first semifinal of the Eurovision Song Contest includes a comedic musical interlude about the differences between Austria and Australia and sends 10 countries through to the grand final on Saturday.

image.altText: three singers in yellow and black jumpsuits clap on stage

items.title: Plan for Trump Tower on Gold Coast scrapped as brand labelled 'toxic'

children.content: The branded tower proposed for Surfers Paradise would have been Australia's tallest building.

image.altText: A skyline with a very tall Trump Tower in the middle.

items.title: Australian company Heytesbury buys mega fleet of 11 live export ships

children.content: Owned by the Holmes à Court family, Heytesbury runs 165,000 head of cattle in northern Australia and has just struck a deal with Vroon to buy its ships.

image.altText: A large grey live export ship docked at a port.

items.title: Australian-born MP at centre of 'palace coup' plot to knife British PM

children.content: Up until the past few days, Catherine West kept a low profile, as far as politicians go. Most Brits would never have come across the Australian-born, London-based MP before, but that all changed at the weekend.

image.altText: A woman smiles at the camera.

items.title: How fuel prices have impacted car sales

children.content: Electric vehicles now represent 16 per cent of new car sales in Australia, in a growing trend coinciding with record fuel prices.

gallery.altText: A line of vehicles in a car park

thumbnail.altText: A line of vehicles in a car park

items.title: Farmers and environmentalists label federal budget 'savage' and 'tragic'

children.content: Weed and pest control programs have been cut in the federal budget as farmers and conservationists grapple with feral animals and noxious plants.

items.title: Wil Anderson hates advertising. So why does he host a show about it?

children.content: It's been 18 years of hosting Gruen for Wil Anderson, but the self-confessed advertising cynic says the topic is more relevant than ever.

image.altText: Wil Anderson in a suit smiling on a television set with a white mug in front of him.

items.title: My four-year break from booze has led to an unexpected discovery

children.content: What started out as abstinence during a pregnancy four years ago turned into a game with myself to see how long I could go without alcohol.

gallery.altText: Natalie crouches down next to a grey kangaroo, and she's smiling.
Natalie on the right, with her arm around her friend and colleague, Erin. They're both smiling.
Two glasses of wine and water sit on a table, by the sea.

thumbnail.altText: Natalie crouches down next to a grey kangaroo, and she's smiling.
Natalie on the right, with her arm around her friend and colleague, Erin. They're both smiling.
Two glasses of wine and water sit on a table, by the sea.

items.title: Voss says Carlton in a better place than when he took charge

children.content: Michael Voss has struck a defiant tone after leaving Carlton, insistent the AFL club is in better shape than when he started as coach.

image.altText: AFL coach walking off the field, looking down at his watch

items.title: Australia axes quick as Women's T20 World Cup squad is named

children.content: Brisbane Heat left-arm pace bowler Lucy Hamilton has been selected in Australia's T20 World Cup squad with the experienced Darcie Brown a surprise omission.

image.altText: Female fast bowler running through after delivering the ball

items.title: 'Got no friends left': Jewish teen bullied while playing Minecraft

children.content: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion hears a 15-year-old was subjected to antisemitic slurs while playing the popular video game with students from a Perth school.

image.altText: A school student holds a phone with the opening menu of the MineCraft mobile app open.

items.title: WA on track for net-zero failure, Woodside-commissioned report warns

children.content: Woodside-commissioned modelling suggests WA is not on track to reach net zero by 2050, even if its massive Browse gas project proceeds, unless renewable energy is rolled out at unprecedented speed.

image.altText: Close up of a sign fastened to a metal pole which reads: "Woodside Energy". Red rocks and two balls of flame in the distance.

items.title: Hundreds of pregnant sheep reported stolen from WA farm

children.content: A farmer in Western Australia's Great Southern region has lost about $100,000 after someone stole hundreds of his pregnant sheep.
